title: Acer polymorphum sanguineum
intro: Acer polymorphum sanguineum, also known as the Sanguine Maple or Blood-red Maple, is a deciduous shrub or small tree belonging to the Sapindaceae family, native to East Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate conditions such as fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Acer polymorphum sanguineum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction, tea, or infusion, and is also used in combination with other herbs to create tonics, extracts, and infusions for various health benefits.
taxonomy: Acer polymorphum sanguineum is classified as a cultivar of the species Acer platanoides, commonly known as the Norway maple. This cultivar is known for its deep red or purple foliage, which is a result of its unique genetic makeup. As a cultivar, Acer platanoides 'Sanguineum' is a human-made variant of the species, bred for its distinctive coloration. The species Acer platanoides is a member of the family Sapindaceae, although some sources may group it as part of the family Aceraceae. The cultivar Acer platanoides 'Sanguineum' is often used as an ornamental tree in landscaping due to its vibrant fall colors.

properties: Acer polymorphum sanguineum, also known as the blood-leaved maple, is said to have various therapeutic properties, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness. Traditional folk remedies have used its extracts to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The plant's leaves and bark are rich in flavonoids, tannins, and other compounds with potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. These compounds may contribute to the plant's reported therapeutic benefits, but more scientific studies are necessary to establish its safety and efficacy. constituents: Acer polymorphum sanguineum contains active constituents such as saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. These compounds have been shown to exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. The saponins present in the plant may also contribute to its potential cardiovascular benefits and anti-cancer effects.
